diet_advice.current_diet_assessment: Daily consumption of soft drinks and junk foods directly undermines the low carbohydrate diet and contributes to severely elevated HbA1c and dyslipidemia.
priority_changes: Completely eliminate soft drinks and junk food from your diet immediately.
Increase daily intake of lean protein sources and non-starchy vegetables.
Replace high-carb grains like white rice and potato with lower-carb alternatives.
diet_advice.breakfast: 2-egg omelette with 1 cup mixed vegetables (onion, tomato, capsicum) and a small handful of groundnuts — Provides protein, healthy fats, and fiber while keeping carbohydrates low.
diet_advice.lunch: 1 cup chicken/fish curry (without potato) with 1 small bowl of palak paneer or mixed vegetable sabzi — Offers essential protein and non-starchy vegetables, supporting blood sugar control.
diet_advice.dinner: 2 small moong dal cheelas (made with grated cabbage/carrot) with 1 small bowl curd — Provides protein and fiber from lentils and vegetables, lower in carbohydrates and easily digestible.
foods_to_avoid: Soft drinks and packaged juices — they are primary contributors to high blood sugar and poor lipid profile.
Sweets, pastries, biscuits, and deep-fried snacks — these foods directly raise blood sugar and contain unhealthy fats.
White rice, white bread, idli, dosa, and potato — these are high glycemic index foods that cause rapid blood sugar spikes.
diet_advice.clinical_note: Patient has stopped all oral antidiabetic medications and statin, leading to severe HbA1c worsening and dyslipidemia; urgent medical review is needed.
